name: Marrakech
cultural_note: When visiting Marrakech, bargaining in the souks is secondary to mutual respect. Start with a friendly 'Salam Alaykum', ask for prices respectfully, and enjoy the bargaining process. Dressing modestly is highly appreciated.

attractions.name: Jemaa el-Fnaa
attractions.description: A legendary, sprawling square that morphs at twilight into an open-air theater filled with storytellers, musicians, and aromatic food stalls.

attractions.name: Jardin Majorelle
attractions.description: A botanical oasis designed by painter Jacques Majorelle, featuring electric Cobalt blue villa, towering cacti, and bamboo pathways.

budget.name: Riad Diana
budget.amenity: Traditional patio, dip pool & tea greeting

luxury.name: La Mamounia
luxury.amenity: World-famous luxury palace hotel with legendary gardens and spa

transportation.description: Small beige taxis ideal for quick within-city travel. Negotiate or ensure the meter is active.
A historic, elegant carriage ride encircling the old clay ramparts of the Medina.
Public bus routes linking the old Medina to Gueliz and Majorelle.

name: Chefchaouen
cultural_note: Chefchaouen is known for its serene, slow-paced atmosphere. It is custom here to ask for permission before photographing residents or their strikingly blue doorway entries.

attractions.name: The Blue Medina Pathways
attractions.description: Wander through pristine labyrinth alleys completely bathed in calming shades of powder-blue wash.

attractions.name: Ras El Maa Waterfall
attractions.description: A refreshing mountain spring waterfall just outside the ancient walls, where locals gather to socialize.

budget.name: Hostel Baraka
budget.amenity: Bohemian rooftop lounge overlooking the Rif mountains

mid_range.name: Lina Ryad & Spa
mid_range.amenity: Indoor heated pool and hammam looking over the valley

transportation.description: Affordable blue taxis servicing the town and transporting visitors up the Rif hills.
Shared transit vehicles connecting Chefchaouen to Tetouan and Tangier.

name: Fez
cultural_note: Fez is Morocco's spiritual heart. Hire a certified guide when exploring Fes El Bali—it is the world's largest active car-free medina with over 9,000 winding lanes.

attractions.name: Chouara Tannery
attractions.description: A spectacular sensory portal dating back to the 11th century, displaying leather dyeing in clay honeycomb basins.

attractions.name: Al-Qarawiyyin University
attractions.description: Founded in 859 AD, recognized by UNESCO as the oldest continuously operating university in the world.

budget.name: Riad Verus
budget.amenity: Social, trendy riad with sunset terrace yoga classes

mid_range.name: Riad Rcif
mid_range.amenity: Imperial 14th-century merchant palace with royal tilework

luxury.name: Riad Fes - Relais & Châteaux
luxury.amenity: Andalusian grandeur with modern design and a 25-meter lap pool

transportation.description: Red petit taxis servicing the modern Ville Nouvelle to the historic medina gates.
Comfortable train routes linking Fez directly to Rabat, Casablanca, and Marrakech.

name: Essaouira
cultural_note: Called the 'Wind City of Africa', Essaouira offers a laidback coastal welcome. Extremely safe medina to explore. Enjoy the Gnaoua music and maritime scenery.

attractions.name: Skala de la Ville
attractions.description: An awe-inspiring seawall battlement featuring rows of 18th-century brass Spanish cannons guarding the blue Atlantic.

attractions.name: The Fishing Port
attractions.description: A bustling harbor packed with iconic bright blue wooden trawlers, seafood grills, and wheeling gulls.

budget.name: Riad Zahra
budget.amenity: Family-run coastal riad just steps away from the sandy beach

mid_range.name: Riad Mimouna
mid_range.amenity: Oceanfront riad built on cliffside, hear the rolling waves

luxury.name: Heure Bleue Palais
luxury.amenity: Relais & Châteaux property with heated rooftop pool and piano bar

transportation.description: Blue petit taxis operating on a flat-rate fare for any inside-town route.
Premium coach connection between Essaouira and Marrakech rail station.

name: Casablanca
cultural_note: As Morocco's dynamic economic engine, Casablanca is fast and modern. Dress respectfully when visiting the Hassan II Mosque. Use red Petit Taxis and always request metered fares.

attractions.name: Hassan II Mosque
attractions.description: A breathtaking architectural masterpiece built on the ocean's edge, featuring a grand 210m minaret and zellige tilework.

attractions.name: The Habous Quarter
attractions.description: A charming neighborhood created in the 1920s containing stone arches, booksellers, and famous Moroccan bakeries.

budget.name: Hotel Central
budget.amenity: Scenic roof overlooking the historic port and medina

mid_range.name: Kenzi Basma
mid_range.amenity: Centrally-located boutique rooms mixing modern and Moorish designs

luxury.name: Four Seasons Casablanca
luxury.amenity: Opulent seaside resort with beach access and private pools

transportation.description: Red petit taxis. Always ensure the meter (compteur) is turned on immediately.
Comfortable, modern tram system linking major districts quickly.

name: Tangier
cultural_note: The historic 'Gateway to Africa' bridging the Mediterranean and Atlantic Oceans. Tangier is multilingual, literary, and very inviting. Watch the sweeping straits from local cafes.

attractions.name: Hercules Caves
attractions.description: Legendary coastal caves famous for their sea-facing opening that resembles the silhouette map of Africa.

attractions.name: The Kasbah
attractions.description: Clifftop medina fortress overlooking Spain and the Strait of Gibraltar, full of whitewashed pathways.

budget.name: Melting Pot Hostel
budget.amenity: Lively roof overlooking the ferry harbor and Gibraltar view

mid_range.name: Dar Jameel
mid_range.amenity: Guesthouse with beautiful wood carvings and color-tiled paths

luxury.name: El Minzah Hotel
luxury.amenity: Grand historic hotel hosting celebrities with Andalusian style

transportation.description: Sky-blue petit taxis servicing the city. Metered or flat rates.
High-speed rail taking you to Rabat & Casablanca in record times.

name: Rabat
cultural_note: Morocco's elegant, clean, and organized political capital. Rabat has a relaxed, upscale feel in comparison to other major cities. Enjoy walks along the Bouregreg valley marina.

attractions.name: Kasbah of the Udayas
attractions.description: A magnificent fortress on cliffs above the sea, featuring peaceful blue-and-white residential alleys.

attractions.name: Hassan Tower & Mausoleum
attractions.description: Imperial red-stone minaret dating back to 1199 AD alongside the marble mausoleum of King Mohammed V.

budget.name: Medina Social Club
budget.amenity: Restored medina guesthouse hosting regular art and music shows

mid_range.name: Riad Kalaa
mid_range.amenity: 1815-built traditional riad with heated pool and hammam

luxury.name: Sofitel Jardin des Roses
luxury.amenity: Luxurious property with sprawling gardens, spa, and pools

transportation.description: Standard blue passenger taxis. Standard meter pricing.
Pristine modern tram networks crossing Bouregreg river to Salé.

name: Agadir
cultural_note: Famed for its year-long sun and sweeping 10km beach. Rebuilt modernly after a 1960 earthquake. Heavily Amazigh (Berber) in culture, specializing in surfing and argan crafts.

attractions.name: Agadir Oufella Kasbah
attractions.description: Breathtaking medieval ruins on a high hill, supplying unbeatable panoramic views of the entire bay.

attractions.name: Souk El Had
attractions.description: One of Africa's largest urban markets, brimming with spices, fabrics, and pure Moroccan argan oil.

budget.name: Atlantic Hostel
budget.amenity: Beach-social vibe with affordable surfboard renting

mid_range.name: Hotel Timoulay & Spa
mid_range.amenity: Boutique hotel with salted saltwater pool and organic spa

luxury.name: Hyatt Regency Taghazout
luxury.amenity: Stunning luxury oceanfront resort popular with pro surfers

transportation.description: Orange petit taxicabs doing city runs. Standard pricing.
Bus transit connect to Taghazout and northern beach towns.

name: Ouarzazate
cultural_note: Known as the 'Gateway to the Desert', Ouarzazate is Morocco's cinematic core. Explore Hollywood historic sets where Gladiator and Game of Thrones were filmed. Warm desert people.

attractions.name: Ait Benhaddou Kasbah
attractions.description: UNESCO World Heritage fortified clay village, masterpiece of traditional earth architecture.

attractions.name: Atlas Film Studios
attractions.description: A sprawling cinema studio hosting colossal sets from legendary Egyptian, Roman, and desert movies.

budget.name: Cinema Riad
budget.amenity: Movie-themed rooms with classic props, homemade clay tajines

mid_range.name: Dar Rita
mid_range.amenity: Cozy clay guesthouse with starry night-gazing rooftop deck

luxury.name: Le Berbere Palace
luxury.amenity: 5-star resort mimicking ancient clay castles with royal pools

transportation.description: Red petit taxis servicing central town areas. Establish flat rates.
Shared sedan vehicle transport towards valleys and Ait Benhaddou.

name: Merzouga
cultural_note: Home of the majestic Erg Chebbi dunes. Merzouga represents the ultimate Saharan desert experience. Community is Berber and Sahrawi, famous for nomad fires and night star-scapes.

attractions.name: Erg Chebbi Dunes
attractions.description: Striking wind-swept golden dunes rising 150m. Perfect for camel tours and dusk walks.

attractions.name: Khamlia Village
attractions.description: A peaceful desert hamlet where descendants of former Saharan travelers perform spiritual Gnawa songs.

budget.name: Desert Hotel Yasmina
budget.amenity: Clay kasbah lodge directly beside sand dunes and small seasonal lake

mid_range.name: Luxury Desert Camp
mid_range.amenity: Comfortable canvas glamping with hot showers and fireside drumming

luxury.name: Caravan by Habitas
luxury.amenity: Ultra-private desert eco-villas with meditation and yoga decks

transportation.description: Four-wheel drive transports crossing rough tracks to luxury dune camps.
The timeless desert transit. Walk over sand ridges at sunrise.

name: Meknes
cultural_note: An imperial capital raised by Sultan Moulay Ismail. Meknes is defined by monumental clay bastions, Spanish-Andalusian doors, and a quieter historical charm in comparison to nearby Fez.

attractions.name: Bab Mansour Gate
attractions.description: North Africa's grandest imperial portal, decorated with monumental marble pillars and zellige tiling.

attractions.name: Roman Ruins of Volubilis
attractions.description: UNESCO-listed ancient Roman ruins featuring remarkably-preserved mosaics, baths, and stone columns.

budget.name: Riad Zahrea
budget.amenity: Cozy courtyard patio serving homebaked flatbreads and mint tea

mid_range.name: Riad D'Or
mid_range.amenity: A plaster-carved ancient merchant house with a shallow pool

luxury.name: Chateau Roslane
luxury.amenity: Splendid resort framed by regional valleys, private gardens

transportation.description: Light blue cabs operating standard routes throughout the city.
A retro carriage ride circling the incredible old city ramparts.

name: Tetouan
cultural_note: A highly refined, artistic city known as the 'White Dove'. Tucked under Mount Dersa, its UNESCO-listed medina features unique Spanish-Andalusian architectural influences with whitewashed streets.

attractions.name: UNESCO Medina of Tetouan
attractions.description: Virtually unaltered medieval city layout with Spanish archways and leather craft bazaars.

attractions.name: School of Traditional Arts
attractions.description: Renowned royal school safeguarding centuries of stucco carvings and zellige patterns.

budget.name: Riad El Reducto
budget.amenity: Charming rebuilt Spanish consulate structure in Moorish tiles

mid_range.name: Riad Blanco
mid_range.amenity: Peaceful white-themed hotel with beautiful tearoom garden

luxury.name: Banyan Tree Tamouda Bay
luxury.amenity: Ultra-luxury seaside private pool villas in M'diq district

transportation.description: Bright yellow cabs servicing central Tetouan neighborhoods.
Shared white sedans linking to sandy beaches of Martil and Fnideq.

name: Al Hoceima
cultural_note: A Mediterranean jewel in the Rif mountains. Famous for pristine white sandy bays, turquoise waters, and fresh local sardine grilling. Deeply hospitable Amazigh community.

attractions.name: Quemado Beach
attractions.description: A magnificent sapphire-green Mediterranean beach cove situated underneath dramatic grey cliffs.

attractions.name: Al Hoceima National Park
attractions.description: Coastal nature reserve combining red limestone cliffs, cedar woodlands, and rare marine reefs.

budget.name: Hotel National
budget.amenity: Pleasant family-run hotel with sea views next to harbor griddle houses

mid_range.name: Mercure Quemado Resort
mid_range.amenity: Cliffside resort looking over Quemado bay, panoramic ocean windows

luxury.name: Radisson Blu Resort
luxury.amenity: Bungalow resort nestled between cedar forests and pristine waves

transportation.description: Sky-blue cabs with white trim. Reliable meter pricing.
Rent standard fishing boats to tour secret sea cliff coves.

name: Ifrane
cultural_note: Nicknamed 'Little Switzerland' due to its crisp alpine climate, red-roofed stone cottages, and heavy winter blizzards. Famously tidy, eco-friendly environment in Atlas Cedar valleys.

attractions.name: The Ifrane Stone Lion
attractions.description: Grand stone-carved lion sculpture created in WWII to pay homage to the historic Atlas lions.

attractions.name: Cedre Gouraud Forest
attractions.description: Ancient woodland inhabited by friendly wild Barbary Macaques, great for nature hiking.

budget.name: Hotel Chamonix
budget.amenity: Alpine chalet style lodge with open fireplace and rich warm stews

mid_range.name: Farah Inn
mid_range.amenity: Splendid stone bungalows with pools, very warm during snowy seasons

luxury.name: Michlifen Resort & Golf
luxury.amenity: Luxe alpine palace offering premier mountain spas and golf courses

transportation.description: Green petit cabs servicing clean avenues of the town.
The perfect green travel alternative to roll down pine-tree lined lanes.

name: Dakhla
cultural_note: Where Sahara's desert ridges face the turquoise Atlantic lagoon waves. Highly famous worldwide for windsurfing. Inherit the Sahrawi tea ceremonies and fresh local oyster meals.

attractions.name: The White Dune
attractions.description: An spectacular, isolated sand ridge standing right inside the turquoise lagoon waves.

attractions.name: Dragon Island
attractions.description: A pristine sandbar island shaped like a sleeping dragon, ideal for hiking during low tide.

budget.name: Dakhla Club Guesthouse
budget.amenity: Surfer-friendly bungalows with direct lagoon entry, board storage

mid_range.name: PK25 Dakhla
mid_range.amenity: Eco-bungalows built on beachfront, glass doors onto kitesurf bays

luxury.name: Dakhla Attitude
luxury.amenity: Luxe windsurf retreat below desert bluffs, beach dinners & spa

transportation.description: 4-wheel drives. The necessary option to traverse sandy beach tracks.
Boat shuttles ferry hikers towards Dragon Island and back.

name: El Jadida
cultural_note: A historical fortress enclave named 'Mazagan' founded by the Portuguese. Renowned for sea gates, massive masonry walls, and subterranean chambers. Friendly Atlantic fishing lifestyle.

attractions.name: Portuguese Cistern
attractions.description: Subterranean vaulted stone chamber reflecting rays of light off shallow waters. Famous film location.

attractions.name: Cite Portugaise Ramparts
attractions.description: Fortified defensive stone walls equipped with old bronze lookouts facing the maritime horizon.

budget.name: Riad Dar Mazagan
budget.amenity: Cozy family-run riad inside old Portuguese fortress walls

mid_range.name: Riad Corail
mid_range.amenity: Oceanward-facing rooms with cool sea breezes and colorful tiling

luxury.name: Mazagan Beach & Golf Resort
luxury.amenity: Massive 5-star ocean compound offering first-class golf spas

transportation.description: Small red taxis. Establish standard pricing before leaving.
A beautiful old carriage ride along coastal harbors and medina.
